page: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance powered by the AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 112 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 56 physical cores with multithreading enabled. It is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding 6.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark evaluations indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-core performance is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Memory performance is highly polarized: small-block cached read bandwidth and PassMark memory scores reach the top 10%, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%. Cryptographic capabilities are highly efficient, with PassMark CPU encryption tests ranking in the top 10%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and VAES instruction sets.

From a resource density perspective, the E112ias_v5 offers high memory capacity and dedicated compute resources suitable for enterprise-scale deployments. The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on external network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs depending on the workload storage profile. The instance provides a high-density, isolated hardware environment, making it a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads requiring strict tenant isolation and massive memory footprints without the overhead of physical hardware management.

This instance is well-suited for memory-intensive database operations, large-scale software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks. Its strong performance in PassMark database operations and Redis SET benchmarks makes it highly appropriate for in-memory caching and relational database hosting. Additionally, its top-tier multi-core execution capabilities support parallelized workloads such as ray tracing, image processing, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 112 dedicated vCPUs and 672.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this server provides 6.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, multi-threaded decompression, and memory scores, alongside strong single-core and cryptographic capabilities. Conversely, uncached large-block memory bandwidth and text processing remain average. Given its isolated architecture and high memory capacity, this instance is highly suited for large-scale database operations, software compilation, and parallelized workloads like ray tracing and image processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 112 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and parallel workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E112ias_v5 is an isolated instance with 112 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 672 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: An isolated, high-capacity virtual machine delivering dedicated AMD EPYC compute and massive memory for demanding enterprise database and multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7763 with 112 dedicated vCPUs and 56 physical cores
Equipped with 672.0 GB of RAM providing 6.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU, encryption, and multi-threaded decompression performance
Isolated hardware profile with Premium Storage capability and no local storage
Optimized for enterprise databases, software compilation, and parallel processing
